purpose: GS LinkOps AI source-evidence delta for database building

records.category: Commercial ground station network / operator capability reference
records.name: SSC Space Global Ground Station Network

service_scope_public: near-Earth, lunar and selected deep-space links
commercial_risk_tags: contracted capacity and SLA not public

records.confidence_level: Medium-High for public network model and regional presence; Medium for exact per-site technical capability because public information is aggregated.
records.next_action: Create SSC provider profile and separate station child records for Esrange, Inuvik, North Pole Alaska, Hawaii, Chile, Thailand and Australia. Mark all station-specific RF/baseband fields as requiring partner or customer confirmation.

records.category: Ground station facility / polar-orbit support benchmark
records.name: Esrange Satellite Station / SSC Space Center

site_role_public: data acquisition and processing for remote sensing and scientific missions
network operations hub for SSC stations

public_capability_indicators.ttc_systems_public: six independent S-band TT&C systems reported in public facility references
public_capability_indicators.receive_systems_public: six multi-frequency S/X-band receive antenna systems reported in public facility references
public_capability_indicators.uhf_note: one S-band TT&C system reported with UHF receive capability in public references

risk_tags: exact antenna inventory requires operator confirmation
commercial access path may depend on SSC contract

records.confidence_level: Medium for specific counts because the count comes from public facility references; High for Esrange as SSC polar station and remote-sensing/TT&C role.
records.next_action: Use as a polar benchmark station. Build a station fingerprint template with public fields filled and technical RF/baseband fields marked as pending confirmation.

records.category: NASA Near Earth Network station / SAR data facility reference
records.name: Alaska Satellite Facility Ground Station
database_targets: SAR Data Reception and Archive Workflow Library

structured_information.operator_or_host: University of Alaska Fairbanks / NASA-supported facility
structured_information.network_context: NASA Near Earth Network member / remote sensing data facility
public_antenna_assets: two 11-meter S/X-band antennas reported in public references
one 9-meter S/X-band antenna reported in public references
service_scope_public: processing and distribution of remote-sensing data
structured_information.data_center_role: NASA DAAC focused on SAR data archive and distribution

records.confidence_level: Medium-High for public antenna class and data-center role; Low for commercial availability because ASF is primarily NASA/science infrastructure rather than commercial GSaaS.
records.next_action: Add ASF as a non-commercial benchmark station for SAR/EO reception workflow and NASA NEN capability comparison, not as an immediately bookable GSaaS station.

records.category: Government network capability matrix / station inventory reference
records.name: NASA Near Earth Network public station capability matrix

public_station_capability_examples: Alaska Satellite Facility: S/X-band, 11m/11m/9m class antennas in public references
Wallops: VHF and S/X-band support in public references
White Sands: VHF and S/Ka-band support in public references
KSAT Singapore/Svalbard/TrollSat: S/X-band station support in public references
SSC Kiruna/Chile/Alaska/Hawaii/Australia: S/X-band support in public references
mission_class: launch and early operations for selected missions

records.confidence_level: Medium because the public matrix is useful for field design but individual station details must be checked against current operator documents.
records.next_action: Use NEN as a reference model for federated station inventory: network -> provider -> station -> antenna asset -> frequency band -> mission/service role.

records.category: ESTRACK / launch and satellite tracking station reference
records.name: Maspalomas Station / INTA / ESTRACK historical station

structured_information.operator_or_host: INTA; historically associated with ESA/NASA support
risk_tags: public role and current availability must be confirmed

records.confidence_level: Medium for public antenna/frequency fields; Low for current third-party access availability.
records.next_action: Add as an ESTRACK/agency benchmark station and mark commercial availability unknown. Use for field taxonomy and station capability schema, not immediate procurement.

records.name: GrADyS-GS: reusable ground station software for field experiments

structured_information.paper_focus: reusable ground-station software framework for controlling, monitoring and storing field-experiment results
architecture_relevance: monitoring and storage of experiment results
gs_linkops_transfer_value: useful reference for modular station-operation UI
supports concept of adapters for heterogeneous assets
risk_tags: not a satellite RF ground-station product
must not be treated as commercial GSaaS capability

records.confidence_level: High for software-architecture inspiration; Low for direct satellite communications capability.
records.next_action: Use only as a software-architecture reference for GS LinkOps AI test manager and adapter layer; do not add it to commercial station inventory.

notes: Run 8 focused on expanding beyond previously captured KSAT/Leaf/AWS/ESA/SatNOGS records by adding SSC global network, Esrange, ASF Fairbanks, NASA NEN matrix normalization, Maspalomas, and one reusable ground-station software architecture paper.
